creating-folders-and-files.label: Creating Folders and Files
creating-folders-and-files.summary: Use mkdir, mkdir -p, and touch to create a useful project structure.

sc-mkdir.title: Which command creates a folder named notes?
sc-mkdir.prompt: Use mkdir with the folder name.
sc-mkdir.hint: Use mkdir with the folder name.

help.concept: Use mkdir with the folder name.
help.hint_1: Use mkdir with the folder name.
help.hint_2: Review the command names from the lesson, then choose the option that matches the task.

sc-touch.title: Which command creates an empty file named todo.txt?
sc-touch.prompt: touch can create an empty file.
sc-touch.hint: touch can create an empty file.

help.concept: touch can create an empty file.
help.hint_1: touch can create an empty file.
help.hint_2: Review the command names from the lesson, then choose the option that matches the task.

sc-mkdir-p.title: Why use mkdir -p?
sc-mkdir-p.prompt: It helps when parent folders may not exist yet.
sc-mkdir-p.hint: It helps when parent folders may not exist yet.

help.concept: It helps when parent folders may not exist yet.
help.hint_1: It helps when parent folders may not exist yet.
help.hint_2: Review the command names from the lesson, then choose the option that matches the task.

mc-create-commands.title: Choose commands that can create workspace items.

ci-create-school-notes.title: Create a school notes structure
ci-create-school-notes.prompt:
Create `school/notes/week1/today.txt`.

Use the terminal. The editor is hidden on purpose for this task.
ci-create-school-notes.hint: Use terminal commands such as `pwd`, `ls`, `mkdir -p`, `touch`, `cp`, `mv`, or `rm` depending on the task.

help.concept: This is a Linux terminal workspace task. Complete it by changing the file tree with terminal commands.
help.hint_1: Use `pwd` and `ls` to inspect where you are.
help.hint_2: The checker looks for the required folders/files and checks that forbidden files are gone.

ci-create-school-notes.placeholder: Use the terminal to complete this task.

mkdir-creates-folders.title: mkdir creates folders
mkdir-creates-folders.bodyMarkdown:
`mkdir` creates a folder.

```bash
mkdir notes
```

If the folder already exists, Linux may show an error. That is normal protection.

mkdir-p-nested.title: mkdir -p creates nested folders
mkdir-p-nested.bodyMarkdown:
`mkdir -p` creates parent folders as needed.

```bash
mkdir -p school/notes/week1
```

This is useful because many tasks require folders inside folders.

touch-creates-files.title: touch creates empty files
touch-creates-files.bodyMarkdown:
`touch` creates an empty file if it does not already exist.

```bash
touch school/notes/week1/today.txt
```

Later you can edit or write into that file, but for many organization tasks, creating it in the right place is the first win.
